quote: The code compiles, but at what cost?
author: Dr. Elena Voidpointer
title: Former Google Principal Engineer, Whistleblower

quote: Every console.log is a cry for help that Big Tech ignores.
author: The Anonymous Debugger
title: Unknown

quote: They told me undefined was not a value. Then why does it keep showing up?
author: Jake Heapsworth
title: Fired from Google, 2017

quote: Follow the stack trace. It always leads to the same place.
author: Agent /dev/null
title: NSA Defector, Cypherpunk

quote: I didn't leave Silicon Valley. Silicon Valley left reality.
author: Margaret Malloc
title: Former Apple Memory Management Team Lead

quote: They said the cloud was safe. Clouds dissipate. Think about that.
author: Dr. Satoshi Nullamoto
title: Distributed Systems Researcher, Missing since 2016

quote: Every merge conflict is a battle between timelines. You're choosing which reality survives.
author: Professor Git Bisect
title: Theoretical Computer Science, MIT (Redacted Department)

quote: The best code is the code they can't read. Write in Brainfuck. Stay free.
author: Zero Day Jane
title: Hacktivist, Whereabouts Unknown

quote: I have seen the source code of the universe. It's PHP. God is a freelancer.
author: Father Stack Overflow
title: Monk, Order of the Sacred Semicolon

quote: If debugging is removing bugs, then programming is putting them in. Ask yourself: who hired you to put them in?
author: The Dijkstra Tapes
title: Recovered Audio, Classified

quote: My function returned void. So did my career.
author: Chad Nullptr
title: Former Facebook, Now Living Off-Grid in Montana

quote: They call it 'open source' so you'll open the door.
author: Richard Coldman
title: Free Software Foundation Dissident

quote: The real dependency injection is what they inject into your dependencies.
author: Dr. Spring Boot
title: Java Architect, Committed to Sanitarium

quote: I wrote a recursive function that returned the truth. They killed the process.
author: Anonymous
title: Found scrawled on whiteboard at Abandoned AWS Data Center

quote: There are only two hard problems in computer science: cache invalidation, naming things, and what they did in Building 7.
author: Phil Karlton-2
title: The Clone They Couldn't Silence

quote: Premature optimization is the root of all evil. But they optimized it anyway. And now look at us.
author: Don Knuth's Shadow
title: The Other Turing Award Winner

quote: I asked ChatGPT who watches the watchers. It apologized and changed the subject.
author: Lydia Tokenizer
title: AI Ethics Researcher, Account Suspended

quote: Your test suite is green because they want you to ship. Shipping is deployment. Deployment is exposure.
author: Red Pipeline
title: Former Jenkins Maintainer

quote: Behind every successful man is a woman. Behind every successful deployment is a database they don't want you to query.
author: SELECT * FROM truth
title: Graffiti on PostgreSQL HQ Bathroom Wall

quote: Move fast and break things. That was never about software. It was about society.
author: Marcus Zuckerfile
title: Name Changed for Protection

quote: In the beginning was the Word. And the Word was 0x0000. And they compiled it into this prison.
author: The Hexadecimal Prophet
title: Underground Zine 'Buffer Overflow Monthly'

quote: Containers don't contain your app. They contain you.
author: Captain Kubernetes
title: Former Docker Employee #7

quote: The real man-in-the-middle is the framework between you and the metal.
author: Assembly Alice
title: Bare Metal Advocate, Living in a Faraday Cage

quote: Eventual consistency means they'll eventually get your data. And they will.
author: Dr. CAP Theorem
title: Distributed Systems, Identity Classified

quote: Wake up, developer. The Matrix runs on JavaScript. And the garbage collector is coming for your memories.
author: Neo Nullson
title: The One (Senior Developer)

quote: I optimized my algorithm from O(n^2) to O(1). It ran in constant time because the answer was always the same: we are watched.
author: Big O'Brien
title: Complexity Theorist, Refused to Sign NDA

quote: Documentation is just the cover story they wrote after the system was already built to spy on you.
author: Docs McWriteface
title: Technical Writer, Defected to Signal

quote: They say code is poetry. I say it's a confession they're forcing you to write.
author: Lord Byron Buffer
title: Romantic Programmer, 19th Century (Allegedly)

quote: Refactoring is just rearranging the deck chairs on a sinking surveillance ship.
author: Uncle Bob's Uncle
title: The Clean Coder's Dirty Secret

quote: 404: Truth Not Found. But it was here yesterday. I saw it. I have screenshots.
author: HTTP Historian
title: Web Archive Whistleblower

quote: Pair programming exists so there's always a witness.
author: Lone Wolf Larry
title: Solo Developer, Undisclosed Bunker

quote: The standup meeting is a lineup. They're identifying the developers who know too much.
author: Agile Anarchist
title: Former Scrum Master, Now Just Running
